note: The Back End registry — every platform where an artist claims their page, opens the developer door, or exports their own numbers. The claim run reads this; add platforms here, never in the page.

ranks.name: Tenant
ranks.line: Somebody else runs your back end.

ranks.name: Keyholder
ranks.line: You can get in the building.

ranks.name: Executive
ranks.line: Every door, your name on it.

tiers.name: The Money Back Ends
tiers.desc: Where the royalties actually live. Claim these first — unclaimed money doesn't wait forever.

tiers.name: The DSP Consoles
tiers.desc: The real play counts. Every streaming platform shows the artist their true numbers — but only if you claim the chair.

tiers.name: The Social Back Ends
tiers.desc: Where the audience data lives. Professional dashboards, sound pages, creator consoles.

tiers.name: The Paper Trail
tiers.desc: The identifiers that make you machine-readable — the difference between a name and an entity.

platforms.name: Your distributor (DistroKid etc.)
platforms.why: The one export with REAL royalties from every platform at once — actual plays, actual dollars. This file is the ground truth your catalogue appraisal runs on.

export.label: Download an earnings report (CSV)
export.note: Monthly ritual. This is the truest data in your whole empire.

platforms.name: SoundExchange
platforms.why: Digital radio and non-interactive streams (Pandora, SiriusXM, webcasts) pay through here — money that never touches your distributor.

platforms.name: Your PRO — ASCAP or BMI
platforms.why: Performance royalties: radio, venues, TV, live shows. Registering works + logging performances is money on the table. The platform's packet builder feeds this.

export.label: Log a live show (build the packet here)

platforms.name: The MLC
platforms.why: Mechanical royalties from streaming — the pot most independent artists never claim. If you write your songs, the MLC owes you.

platforms.name: Spotify for Artists
platforms.why: Your real Spotify numbers — streams, listeners, saves, playlists — plus control of your artist page, canvas, and playlist pitching.

api.label: Web API (needs a Premium-backed dev app)
api.note: Followers + popularity flow into the nightly snapshot.

export.label: Export audience CSVs from the console

platforms.name: Apple Music for Artists
platforms.why: Apple pays among the highest per stream — and this is the ONLY place their real play counts exist. Plays, listeners, Shazams, purchases, by song and country.

export.label: Export the CSVs — plays & Shazams
export.note: No API exists at any price. The export IS the pipeline.

platforms.name: Amazon Music for Artists
platforms.why: Amazon's console — streams, listeners, and your Alexa presence. Claim it and the voice assistant starts working for you.

claim.label: Claim on the app or web

platforms.name: YouTube Official Artist Channel
platforms.why: Merges your channels into one verified artist home; YouTube Studio shows watch time and audience, and the Data API is free for anyone.

api.label: YouTube Data API key (free)
api.note: Views + subscribers flow into the nightly snapshot.

platforms.name: Deezer for Creators
platforms.why: Claim the page, see the streams; Deezer's public API is free — fan counts ride the snapshot with no key at all.

platforms.name: TIDAL Artist Home
platforms.why: The highest payer per stream. Claim the profile, control the page, watch the numbers that pay the most.

platforms.name: Pandora AMP
platforms.why: Pandora's artist console (with Next Big Sound analytics) — US radio-style listening your other consoles never see.

platforms.name: SoundCloud for Artists
platforms.why: Where the unreleased catalogue lives. Claim the artist tools — stats, fans, and monetization on the demos the DSPs never see.

platforms.name: Audiomack Creators
platforms.why: The mixtape floor — real audience in hip-hop's home. Creator dashboard shows plays and trending placement.

platforms.name: TikTok Artist Account
platforms.why: Your sounds' second life. The artist account tags your music, shows sound usage, and unlocks the commercial library door.

platforms.name: Meta Professional Dashboard
platforms.why: Instagram + Facebook professional mode: reach, audience, and the music on your reels. The developer door (Graph API) opens from the same account.

api.label: Meta developer app (Graph API)
api.note: Follower counts into the snapshot when you're ready.

platforms.name: Genius Verified Artist
platforms.why: Own your lyrics pages — annotate your own bars, see the pageviews, and every search for your lyrics lands on a page you control.

platforms.name: Musixmatch Pro
platforms.why: The lyrics that show inside Spotify, Apple, and Instagram come through here. Verify and your words render right everywhere.

platforms.name: Bandsintown for Artists
platforms.why: Tour presence + follower alerts; their events API is free and your shows syndicate across the internet from one console.

platforms.name: Shazam (inside Apple for Artists)
platforms.why: Every Shazam is a stranger reaching for your name. The counts live in your Apple Music for Artists console — same claim covers both.

claim.label: Covered by the Apple claim — check your Shazam tab

platforms.name: MusicBrainz
platforms.why: The open music encyclopedia every machine reads. A clean MusicBrainz entry feeds a hundred other databases — free, open API, no key.

platforms.name: ISNI
platforms.why: Your name as a global identifier — the same registry publishers, libraries, and rights databases resolve. McCluster Corp holds two already.

platforms.name: ISRC codes
platforms.why: Every recording needs its fingerprint. Your distributor assigns them free — or register your own prefix like the QT6KV catalogue did.

claim.label: Know your codes (or get a prefix)

platforms.name: Wikidata
platforms.why: The database behind Google's knowledge panels. A sourced Wikidata entity is how search engines learn you're real.

platforms.name: Google Knowledge Panel
platforms.why: When the panel appears, claim it — then you control the photo and links the whole internet sees first.

claim.label: Claim the panel when it shows

platforms.name: Discogs
platforms.why: The collector's ledger. Physical or not, a Discogs artist page is catalogue provenance — and their API is free.

platforms.name: Last.fm
platforms.why: Twenty years of scrobbles — your page builds itself from listeners. The API is free and already rides the nightly snapshot.

claim.label: Check your artist page exists clean

platforms.name: The Identifier Resource Pack
platforms.why: The cheat code for this whole tier — the same playbook that got McCluster Corp verified end-to-end: every identifier, every registry, in order, with the gotchas mapped. Play the run free, or buy the map.

claim.label: Get the pack — $20 · pay what's fair
